article_meta.title: Administration Trump says there will ‘likely be more’ American deaths in Iran before conflict ends by Max Rego - 03\/01\

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: Administration Trump says there will ‘likely be more’ American deaths in Iran before conflict ends by Max Rego - 03\/01\
SOURCE: The Hill

ARTICLE:

Administration Trump says there will ‘likely be more’ American deaths in Iran before conflict ends by Max Rego - 03\/01\/26 6:00 PM ET by Max Rego - 03\/01\/26 6:00 PM ET Share ✕ LinkedIn LinkedIn Email Email NOW PLAYING President Trump spoke about the ongoing U.S. operation against Iran on Sunday afternoon and acknowledged it is “likely” that more U.S. service members will die. Earlier Sunday, U.S. Central Command (Centcom) said that as of 9:30 a.m. EST, three American service members have been killed in action, while five were injured and “several” others sustained minor shrapnel injuries and concussions during the operation that the U.S. and Israel launched against Iran on Saturday. “We pray for the full recovery of the wounded and send our immense love and eternal gratitude to the families of the fallen,” the president said in a video on his Truth Social platform. “And sadly, there will be likely be more, before it ends. That’s the way it is. “[There will] likely be more but we’ll do everything possible where that won’t be the case.” The U.S. launched “Operation Epic Fury” at 1:15 a.m. EST Saturday, deploying stealth B-2 bombers; F-16, F-18 and F-22 fighter jets; and M-142 high mobility artillery rocket systems, among other assets, according to Centcom. The command said Sunday that in the first 24 hours of the operation, the U.S. targeted Iranian command and control centers; the joint and aerospace forces headquarters of the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC); integrated air defense systems; ballistic missile sites, Iranian navy ships and submarines; anti-ship missile sites; and military communication capabilities. Centcom shared videos of the U.S. striking IRGC headquarters and Iranian ballistic missile sites on the social platform X. Trump also said the U.S. sank nine Iranian navy ships and Iranian naval buildings. The joint U.S. and Israeli strikes resulted in the death of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, along with a slew of political and military officials in the Middle Eastern country. Trump celebrated Khamenei’s death on Sunday, calling him a “wretched and vile man” who “had the blood of hundreds, even thousands, of Americans on his hands.” In the wake of Khamenei’s death, an interim leadership council composed of Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, Guardian Council jurist member Ayatollah Alireza Arafi and head of the Iranian judiciary Gholam Hossein Mohseni-Eje’i is now leading Iran, according to Iranian state television Press TV. But Trump on Sunday reiterated his call for the IRGC, the armed forces and police to “lay down your arms” or “face certain death.” The president also urged the Iranian people to “seize this moment” and take control of their country. The Islamic Republic has governed Iran for more than four decades, repeatedly crushing dissent — including earlier this year, when the government cracked down on antiregime protesters. “Be brave, be bold, heroic and take back your country,” Trump remarked. “America is with you. I made a promise to you and I fulfilled that promise. The rest will be up to you, but we’ll be there to help.” The president also told the Daily Mail on Sunday that the U.S. operation in Iran will take roughly four weeks. “It’s always been a four-week process,” Trump said. “We figured it will be four weeks or so. It’s always been about a four-week process so — as strong as it is, it’s a big country, it’ll take four weeks — or less.” After spending the weekend at his Mar-a-Lago resort in Florida, the president is en route to Washington, D.C. Add as preferred source on Google Tags Ali Khamenei Ayatollah Ali Khamenei Donald Trump Masoud Pezeshkian Copyright 2026 Nexstar Media Inc. All rights reserved. This material may not be published, broadcast, rewritten, or redistributed. ---

  "summary": "President Trump acknowledged that more American service members are likely to die before the ongoing US conflict with Iran ends. US and Israeli forces have launched Operation Epic Fury, targeting multiple Iranian military assets and resulting in the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and other officials. Trump called for the Iranian military and people to seize control of their country. An interim Iranian leadership council has taken over governance. Trump estimated the operation may last about four weeks.",
  "key_facts": [
    "Trump stated more US service member deaths are likely in the Iran conflict before it ends.",
    "Three Americans were killed and several injured in ongoing operations against Iran.",
    "US and Israeli forces launched 'Operation Epic Fury' targeting Iranian military infrastructure.",
    "Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in the joint strikes.",
    "Interim Iranian leadership council formed, and Trump called for Iranians to take control of their country."
  ],
  "sources_represented": ["The Hill"]
}

parsed_output.summary: President Trump acknowledged that more American service members are likely to die before the ongoing US conflict with Iran ends. US and Israeli forces have launched Operation Epic Fury, targeting multiple Iranian military assets and resulting in the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and other officials. Trump called for the Iranian military and people to seize control of their country. An interim Iranian leadership council has taken over governance. Trump estimated the operation may last about four weeks.

Three Americans were killed and several injured in ongoing operations against Iran.
US and Israeli forces launched 'Operation Epic Fury' targeting Iranian military infrastructure.
Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in the joint strikes.
